meaning: n.<美>捆干草用的铁丝,取去干草后的杂乱缠绕的铁丝
examples: After that, things started to go haywire.
Many Americans think their legal system has gone haywire.
But again, Haywire just quietly stares at him.
The world was going haywire and we were sticking to our predetermined path anyway.
Furthermore, when something does go haywire, the speed at which traders react is vastly accelerated.
